title: Validation of the Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire: Does the Traditional Chinese Medicine Concept of Body Constitution Exist?
abstract: The study aims to adapt and validate the Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire (CCMQ) in Hong Kong Chinese people. 10 patients and 10 Chinese medicine practitioners (CMP) confirmed the content validity (CVI: 50%–100%) of CCMQ. 1084 HK subjects completed a cross-sectional study with 98.6% who could be classified into one or more BC types. Scaling success rates were 85.7%–100% for the 9 BC scales. Construct validity was supported by moderate correlations between CCMQ and SF-12v2 scores. The confirmatory factor analysis showed a reproducible structure as hypothesized. People with gentleness BC type had better health-related quality of life, HRQOL, than those with other (imbalanced) BC types. Internal consistency (reliability) (Cronbach’s alpha > 0.6) and test-retest reliability were also satisfactory (ICC > 0.6) for all scales. However, the sensitivity and specificity in predicting the BC types diagnosed by CMP were only fair, ranging from 42.7% to 82.7%. 27.6% of subjects had a change from the imbalanced BC types to gentleness BC type after 6 months. The CCMQ was adapted for HK Chinese people and proved to be valid, reliable, and responsive. People classified to have imbalanced BC types had significantly lower HRQOL than gentleness BC type, which supported the validity and importance of the TCM concept of the physiological BC type.

Sensitivity and specificity were also assessed." vs "The paper provides multiple direct assessments of validity and reliability of the CCMQ as a diagnostic tool for TCM constitution classification."
- Reason: The paper conducts comprehensive validation of the CCMQ, including content, construct, criterion, reliability, and responsiveness analyses—directly evaluating the validity and reliability of the TCM constitution classification method.

title: Validation of the constitution in Chinese medicine questionnaires with the newly developed blood-vacuity category: a rare Taiwanese case study
abstract: The concept of body constitution (BC) is a core notion in traditional Chinese medicine, used in diagnosis, treatment, and prevention; however, there is little standardization in terms of definitions and measurements. To improve standardization, constructive questionnaires have been developed to classify the various BC types. One of the most commonly used is the Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire (CCMQ). Despite including nine BCs, CCMQ lacks the blood-vacuity constitution, although it is often noted in clinical practice. In this study, we have modified the original CCMQ to include the blood-vacuity constitution and amended the language to better suit the Taiwan population. The revised questionnaire was given to a panel of experts to check for content validity, and then distributed to volunteers for completion. The reliability analysis, based on 512 valid questionnaires, achieved a Cronbach's alpha value of 0.65–0.86. The content validity index scores ranged from 60 to 100. In addition, we collected demographic data from our volunteers and found that BMI, gender, exercise frequency, disease status, allergies, and psychiatric disorder status may impact the body constitution. Collectively, our study presents an expanded version of the CCMQ which includes the blood-vacuity constitution, and has been validated among the Taiwanese population. Demographic data also demonstrates possible relationships among BC, lifestyle and diseases.

These metrics are direct assessments of the validity and reliability of the revised CCMQ as a diagnostic tool for TCM constitution classification." vs "The paper must specifically assess or discuss the validity (or reliability) of these classification standards or diagnostic methods."
- Reason: The paper provides explicit quantitative evidence on both reliability (Cronbach’s alpha) and content validity (CVI scores), directly evaluating the psychometric properties of the modified questionnaire used for TCM constitution assessment. title: Suggestion and Analysis on Revise of Standard of Classification and Determination of Constitution in TCM
abstract: Objective The standard of Classification and Determination of Constitution in TCM has been published in April,2009 and applied in national test units for 4 years.The paper investigated the revise necessity of the standard and provided suggestions and thinking train for revise direction through surveying the application of the standard.The purpose is to further improve the sensitivity and contribution of the standard.Methods Taken the constitution scale in the standard as specific object,the questionnaire of application suggestion to the scale was issued to 130 national centers of preventive treatment of diseases and the feedback was collected,the reviews on the structure and content of the scale were collected from experts of related disciplines,and questionnaire was issued to common people for collecting the opinions in the application of the scale.All data was analyzed by using statistical method.Results After analyzing 30 pieces of questionnaire collected from national centers of preventive treatment of diseases,the results showed that the total structure of the scale was accepted by common people,but impalpable words were major questions in the scale.The on-site consultation in related experts showed that the content validity and construct validity reached the level required by the scale,and the numbers of items could be reduced,some describing modes could be changed and calculation method of constitutional types could be simplified.The survey in common people showed that the difference in age and educational degree had influences on filling questionnaire,and calculating and understanding of judging results.The outcomes had significance after statistical analysis(P0.05).Conclusion After spreading and application in national range for several years,the standard of Classification and Determination of Constitution in TCM has been well received by community hospitals,centers of preventive treatment of diseases and the State Administration of Traditional Chinese Medicine.However,hospitals,common people and experts in related disciplines have given revise suggestions and feedbacks.It is feasible and necessary to revise and adjust the standard partly.

title: Questions on Standard ofClassiifcation and Determination ofPhysical Constitutions in TCM
abstract: The standard ofClassificationandDetermination of Physical Constitutions in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM), which was issued by the China Association of TCM in 2009, has been promoted and implemented. However, we found that the criteria were inconsistent with the principles and methods of constitution classification in TCM after serious research. The features for physical constitution classification were repetitive and redundant without clear boundaries among them. Furthermore, there were poor correlations among different factors within a single physical constitution. Accordingly, it was suggested that the theory of physical constitutions in TCM should not be confused with and treated as the science of it. Because the standard ofClassiifcationandDetermination of Physical Constitutions in TCM is not mature enough, we should be cautious when applying it.

title: Primary compiling of Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire
abstract: Aim: To develop a standard measure for assessing the constitutional types of Chinese medicine. Methods: The Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire (CCMQ) was developed based on the previous researches and focus group discussion (FGD). Under the guidance of theories of constitution in Chinese Medicine, we conducted a series of work, including establishing the concept of constitutional types frame, collecting items, forming pool of items, selecting questions and items, and several pilot studies and evaluating. Results: We developed the 60-item CCMQ, which could be self-assessed and could be used by interview. The CCMQ consisted of nine sub-scales: Gentleness type, Qi-deficiency type, Yang-deficiency type, Yin-deficiency type, Phlegm-wetness type, Wetness-heat type, Blood-stasis type, Qi-depression type, Special diathesis type. Conclusion: This CCMQ could be used to evaluate constitutional types in individuals and populations, and could supply a standard scale in the classification research of the constitution in Chinese medicine.

title: Decision Making Model of "Five Elements of the Physical Constitution"
abstract: Based on the"Five element of physical constitution"and"Yin and Yang of Chinese medical theory",people will be classified as the five types. Its judgment method rests on person's build,the psychological feature,the physique characteristic and the Chinese medicine diagnosis and so on. Nowadays, Chinese medicine has developed some quantity assessment tools in classifying the five elements of the physical constitution. However,comparing with the modern West psychology,the current assessment tool still requires a lot of improvement in objective and the accuracy. Traditional Chinese medical doctors have used to diagnosis subjectively with the deficient uniform. To establish a new assessment model with the effective objective and high accuracy is the most critical to lead a solid scientific approach to the traditional Chinese medicine. The purpose of this study is to establish a decision making model of"Five element of physical constitution". Through the expert interview and AHP(Analytic Hierarchy Process)method,this model will provide the methodology and systematization structure to weight the different values of evaluated factories to improve the accuracy of"Five Elements of the Physical Constitution"evaluation.

title: Constitutional classification and comprehensive evaluation in Chinese medicine
abstract: The constitution in traditional Chinese medicine(TCM) theory believes that,constitution is formed before birth and can be improved after birth,it is an integrated,relatively stable inherent characteristic which is composed of morphology,physiological function and psychological state in one,s life.Constitutional classification of Chinese medicine is based on the individual different morphological structure,physiological function,psychological state and so on,according to certain standards,using a certain method,we collate,analyze and summarize relevant information,and then classify constitution into several types.Since each individual is composed of the Essence,Qi,Blood,Body fluid and so on,and these fundamental substances interact with each other,in real life and medical practice,though we can find some typical constitutional type,the constitutional characteristics of most people are not typical,mixed constitution is widespread among the majority of the crowd,and the type and extent of mixed constitution also vary individually.To this end,based on the constitutional classification theory of TCM,we have developed a standard measurement tool(Constitution in Chinese Medicine Questionnaire) to determine constitutional classification of Chinese medicine,and we will explore the application of this tool further,so that we can provide a simple,effective and intuitive method which can comprehensively evaluate the mixed constitution for clinical practice.

abstract: This article mainly elaborated around the four common categories of objective classification indicators, including constitution-gene and pathway, symptoms and signs, imaging examinations and biological indicators. title: Information-Technology-Based Analysis of Chinese Medicine Constitutions of Residents
abstract: Objective:By using the information technology to study the constitutional types of Chinese medicine in 2500 residents above the age of 15 living in Baiyun Street of Yuexiu District of Guangzhou;to analyze the distribution regularity of their constitutional types of Chinese medicine and obtain the assessed conversion data between 9 constitutional types among people. Methods:According to Classification and Determination of Constitution in TCM issued by China Association of Chinese Medicine,relying on the self-developed analysis system of the constitutional types of Chinese medicine in community population, systematic sampling and questionnaire methods were used for this investigation. Results:The first three types of constitution were gentleness type(40.7%),Yang-deficiency type(13.8%)and Qi-deficiency type(11.4%). The top three mean values of conversion score were:gentleness type(58.70±15.10),Qideficiency type(32.33±17.92)and Yang-deficiency type(29.89±20.85). These data had a statistical significant difference(P0.05)comparing with similar investigations in China;the composition ratios of constitutional types in different gender and age were different with a statistical significant difference(P0.05);33% of gentleness-typed people had pathological constitution tendency;the composition ratios of pathological constitution tendency types in different gender were different but there was no statistical significant difference(P=0.357). Conclusion:Among the residents over the age of 15 in Baiyun Street community,gentleness type accounted for 40.7%;eight pathological constitutions accounted for 59.3%; Yang-deficiency type and Qi-deficiency type were more common in pathological constitution,and these two types had a much higher significant degree comparing with other pathological constitutions;the constitutional characteristics were varied according to different genders and ages;about 1/3 of gentleness-typed people had different pathological constitution tendency,and there was no significant difference between men and women.

title: A Study of Traditional Chinese Medicine Body Constitution Associated with Overweight, Obesity, and Underweight
abstract: Objective . The aim of the study was to investigate the associations among the nine types of body constitution in traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) with the outcomes of overweight, obesity, and underweight. Method . Participants aged 30 to 90 years were recruited from communities in Shanghai and assessed using a self-administered questionnaire pertaining to their demographics, lifestyles, and self-reported medical history. The data of 3748 participants with complete information was available for the analysis. Multinomial logistic regression (MLR) analysis was performed to determine the associations among the TCM constitution variables and the health outcomes. Results . The standards of classification and determination of the constitution in TCM were used to gauge the patients’ constitution type. MLR revealed independent and significant associations among the Qi_Deficient and Yang_Deficient groups with the outcomes of overweight, obesity, and underweight ( P < 0.10 for all). MLR revealed independent and significant associations among the Qi_Deficient and Yang_Deficient groups with the outcomes of overweight, obesity, and underweight ( P < 0.05 for all). Conclusion . Our study revealed significant negative correlations between the Qi_Deficient and Yang_Deficient groups with the outcomes of overweight, obesity, and underweight. On the other hand, positive correlations were found between Phlegm_Dampness and the outcomes of overweight and obesity.

title: A Study on Men's 8-Constitutional Characteristics Using the Oneway Analysis of Variance
abstract: In Oriental Medicine, there have been a number of researches in 8-Constitution medicine which classifies human bodies into one of 8 categories. Since the diagnosis of 8-Constitution depending on pulse types is often subjective, standardization of the diagnosis and therapy requires objective characteristics of 8 constitutions. A questionnaire of 80 items about personal symptoms were given to patients while they were classified into one of 8 constitutions based on their pulse types and responses to constitution-based acupuncture therapy. One-way analysis of variance and Duncan's multiple comparison test were used to verify the significant items. Most of the significant items in this study coincided with supposed-to-be clinical characteristics of 8-constitutions.

title: Investigation and analysis of Chinese medicine constitutions identification among sub-health of 689 cases
abstract: Objective To provide the new ideas of sub-health population health and health care through Chinese traditional medicine identification of 689 sub-health cases, which aimed to understand their physical distribution condition. Methods According to classification and judgment of the Association of Chinese Medicine TCM constitution, TCM constitution recognition investigation was implemented by using of the questionnaire. Results Among 689 sub-health cases, 77 cases were Gentleness type (11.18%), 612 cases of Biased constitution(88.82%). To arrange in number of people with biased physical, 242 cases were Yang-deficiency type (39.54%), 93 cases Yin-deficiency type (15.20%), 78 cases Qi-deficiency type (12.75%), 75 cases Qi-depression type (12.25%), 42 cases Went-heat type (6.86%), 36 cases Blood-stasis type (5.88%), 23 cases Special diathesis type (3.76%), 23 cases phlegm-wetness type (3.76%). Conclusion Combined with physical identification, establishing individual scheme of constitution recuperation has positive significance on sub-health population health and health care.
